name: Capetown Fruit & Vegetable Curry
recipeIngredient: 2 Firm, tart apples, cored and cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown and translucent, about 10 minutes.
2. Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, ground cumin seeds, coriander seeds, cinnamon, turmeric, cayenne, fennel seeds, cardamom, and cloves. Cook for an additional 3 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
3. Add the zucchini slices and water to the skillet. Stir well so that the spices do not stick to the pot. Cover and reduce heat to low. Simmer gently for 10 minutes.
4. Mix in the green beans, apples, peppers, and dried apricots. Continue simmering, covered, stirring occasionally, until all the vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes. Add a little more water if needed to prevent sticking.
5. Stir in the raisins, conserve, and lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as desired. If you prefer it spicier, add more cayenne or garam masala. For added sweetness, include more conserve; for tanginess, add extra lemon juice.
6. Serve over a bed of rice, garnished with nuts and sliced banana.
